novel_features.name: Recheck delta cursor
novel_features.description: Return only the filings filed since a given timestamp, using a per-CIK local cursor — the entire LODESTAR /$recheck loop in one call.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local SQLite filings table with last_seen_accession; no SEC endpoint returns 'what's new since DATE.'
novel_features.why_it_matters: For quarterly thesis rechecks, reach for this before fetching the full submissions index — it eliminates re-paying token cost on filings already seen.

novel_features.name: 8-K material-item enumeration
novel_features.description: Enumerate 8-Ks with parsed Item numbers and a --material-only flag that excludes exhibits-only (Item 9.01 alone) refilings.
novel_features.rationale: Requires 8-K body parse for Item-number taxonomy; the SEC endpoint returns body HTML, not the structured Item list with material classification.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this instead of pulling 8-K bodies when you need 'has anything material happened?' — answers in one call without reading filing text.

novel_features.name: Insider follow-through join
novel_features.description: For every senior-officer code-S sale of ≥$1M, scan the next 90 days of 8-Ks for material items and emit (sale, subsequent material 8-K, days-between) pairs.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join of insider_transactions × filings by date proximity; no SEC endpoint returns this correlation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this in LODESTAR Gate 2 execution-validation when an insider sale precedes material disclosures — surface management exits before bad news.

novel_features.name: Cross-sectional XBRL pivot
novel_features.description: Multi-ticker XBRL pivot that resolves concept aliases (Revenues ↔ RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax ↔ SalesRevenueNet) into a flat ticker×quarter×concept table.
novel_features.rationale: companyfacts is single-ticker and exposes raw concept names; same economic quantity reports under 3-4 different US-GAAP tags depending on filer's accountant.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For cross-sectional quality screens — pivot before parsing 50 companyfacts JSON blobs by hand.

novel_features.name: Item-scoped section extraction
novel_features.description: Extract requested Items from a 10-K or 10-Q with byte-offset boundaries; emits ONLY the requested items in compact JSON instead of the full 100KB-10MB HTML body.
novel_features.rationale: SEC returns the full undifferentiated HTML body; agent consumers need item-scoped output to stay within context budget. Anchored on the Item NA regex with byte offsets.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this instead of fetching the raw 10-K body — saves an order of magnitude in tokens when you only need Risk Factors and MD&A.

novel_features.name: Ownership-cross enumeration
novel_features.description: Enumerate 13D and 13G filings against an issuer (when someone else crosses 5% of the ticker), with filer name, percent owned, and filed-at.
novel_features.rationale: Service-specific 5%-cross taxonomy; submissions index filter to 13D/G/-A plus cover-page percent parse, which no community wrapper exposes structured.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in LODESTAR Gate 3 asymmetric-structure checks to spot activist or institutional concentration without scrolling submissions.

novel_features.name: Governance red flags
novel_features.description: Compose three independent service-specific signals into one call: 8-K Item 4.01 auditor changes, Item 4.02 non-reliance restatements, and NT-10-K late-filing notices (Form 12b-25).
novel_features.rationale: No wrapper bundles these three signals; they map directly onto LODESTAR Gate 2 immediate disqualifiers and need to be a one-call check during research and recheck.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as an early disqualifier check — if any flag fires, surface to LODESTAR before spending tokens on the full thesis.

novel_features.name: Offline FTS over cached bodies
novel_features.description: Full-text search over locally-cached filing bodies via FTS5 — ticker- and form-scoped — with snippet windows and byte offsets for precise re-read.
novel_features.rationale: Distinct from `search` (which hits efts.sec.gov): never re-hits the SEC endpoint for cached bodies; token-cheap for repeated queries during a single research session.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for fts during deep-dives where you re-query the same filing body multiple times; reach for the absorbed `search` command for the first lookup or for cross-issuer queries.

narrative.headline: An agent-native CLI for SEC EDGAR — six sanctioned endpoints, SQLite-cached filings with FTS5, and LODESTAR-shaped compound bundles (primary-sources, insider-summary) that no other EDGAR tool emits.
narrative.value_prop: Built for a Claude Code agent doing conviction research on public-company filings. Compound commands collapse a ticker's full primary-source pull into one structured response; insider-summary distinguishes discretionary code-S sales from RSU-tax code-F noise and flags senior officers separately. Local SQLite cache with tiered TTLs makes re-reads near-free; FTS5 over cached bodies eliminates re-hitting EDGAR for the same query.
narrative.auth_narrative: No API key — SEC EDGAR is publicly accessible. Identity is the User-Agent: set COMPANY_PP_CONTACT_EMAIL once and every request goes out as `lodestar-edgar-pp-cli <email>`. The CLI refuses to run if the env var is unset, with a clear error pointing at setup. Rate-limited to ≤2 req/sec sustained (well under SEC's 10 req/sec ceiling) with adaptive backoff on 429.

quickstart.comment: Verifies COMPANY_PP_CONTACT_EMAIL is set (required for SEC fair-access User-Agent), runs a reachability probe against data.sec.gov, opens the SQLite store, confirms FTS5.
LODESTAR primary-source bundle: latest 10-K, four 10-Qs, 90-day 8-Ks, 12-month Form 4s with senior-officer flag, latest DEF 14A — all in one structured response.
Form 4 aggregator with code-S (discretionary) vs code-F (RSU tax) separated and senior officers flagged.
Recheck delta — only filings filed after the supplied timestamp, using the local cursor.

troubleshoots.fix: export COMPANY_PP_CONTACT_EMAIL=user[at]example.com — SEC fair-access requires identification in the User-Agent.
The 2 req/sec limiter is conservative but adaptive backoff is in play; if persistent, your IP may be in a 10-minute SEC penalty box — wait, then retry.
Drop --offline or run edgar-pp-cli sync <TICKER> first to warm the local SQLite store.

troubleshoots.symptom: Wrong ticker → CIK after corporate action

troubleshoots.fix: Use --senior-only and verify the column is is_discretionary; code F (RSU tax withholding) is non-directional and should be excluded from net sells.

narrative.when_to_use: Use edgar-pp-cli when an agent needs structured SEC filing data shaped for token-efficient consumption — primary-source bundles, insider-summary with S/F discrimination, cross-quarter MD&A diffs, or local FTS5 over cached filings. The compound primary-sources command is the LODESTAR shape: one call returns the full evidence pack for a Gate 1/2/3 thesis. Do not use this CLI for Federal Register searches, regulations.gov dockets, activist short reports, or NASDAQ short interest — those are out of scope and handled elsewhere.

recipes.title: Full LODESTAR research pull
recipes.explanation: Compact JSON for an agent — primary-sources returns the full bundle; --select narrows to only the fields LODESTAR Gate evaluations actually need, dropping boilerplate.

recipes.title: Quarterly recheck delta
recipes.explanation: Only filings filed after the supplied timestamp. Local cursor — no SEC round-trip when the cache is warm. Pair with eightk-items --material-only when the delta includes 8-Ks.

recipes.title: Insider follow-through pattern
recipes.explanation: Cross-entity join: senior-officer code-S sales of ≥$1M paired with subsequent material 8-K items within 90 days. Surfaces management exits before bad news.

recipes.title: Cross-sectional XBRL screen
recipes.explanation: Multi-ticker pivot with concept-alias resolution — flat table for downstream screening or spreadsheet load. Use --csv for tabular pipes.

recipes.title: Token-efficient 10-K item extraction
recipes.explanation: Pulls only Risk Factors (1A), MD&A (7), and Quantitative Disclosures (7A) with byte offsets — order of magnitude fewer tokens than the full filing body.

recipes.title: Local FTS5 deep-dive
recipes.explanation: Offline FTS5 over cached filing bodies; emits snippet + byte offsets for precise re-read. Run sync first if cache is cold.
